exemplar.title: Modelling the volume of a Coca-Cola Contour Bottle

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the volume of a Coca-Cola Contour Bottle

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear linear narrative with a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure
Title and scope are clearly stated, establishing a professional presentation
Final assembly of the piecewise function is presented coherently, reinforcing logical development
weaknesses: Lengthy algebraic transcriptions and screenshots interrupt the flow and reduce conciseness
Several tables (e.g., Tables 6 and 7) are corrupted or mis-formatted, breaking coherence
Inconsistent figure and table numbering and misaligned headers detract from the organization
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 is appropriate: the report is coherent and logically developed with clear organization, but repetition, overlong algebraic detail, and formatting issues prevent it from reaching the highest level of conciseness and polish.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation (e.g., LaTeX-rendered Lagrange formula)
Effective use of multiple representations: algebraic expressions, colour-coded Desmos graphs, tables, and a 3D GeoGebra model
Good logical structure and clear narrative guiding the reader through data â interpolation â integration
weaknesses: Minor unit inconsistencies (cm vs m) and one or two mistyped coefficients
Corrupted tables and formatting glitches obscure some numeric values
Overlong formulae at times hamper immediate clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication is mostly consistent and clear, with effective multiple representations and accurate notation. Minor glitches prevent a flawless âexcellentâ rating, but overall performance warrants band 4.

strengths: Personal narrative linking childhood interest to the modelling task enhances engagement
Iterative refinement of polynomial fits and creation of a 3D model demonstrate initiative
Clear justification for methodological choices (piecewise vs single function) shows independent thinking
weaknesses: Reliance on standard interpolation techniques and online calculators limits conceptual innovation
Approach, while personal, follows a familiar IA pathway without groundbreaking elements
Could extend design or data-collection methods to deepen personal involvement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 2 reflects significant evidence of independent thinking, a personal approach, and good creativity, but not the outstanding innovation required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement uncertainty and model-assumption limitations
Thoughtful analysis of discrepancies between calculated and nominal volumes
Comprehensive evaluation including future validation suggestions (water displacement) and discussion of methodological implications
weaknesses: Lacks a rough quantification of groove-volume impact to support assumptions
Limited discussion of error propagation reliability for the interpolation method
Could deepen insight by comparing alternative modelling approaches in more detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection demonstrates substantial critical thought, deep analysis of results and process, and a clear, evaluative discussion of outcomes with future implications, fitting band 3.

strengths: Comprehensive application of polynomial interpolation and piecewise modelling to fit complex bottle geometry
Appropriate and well-justified use of definite integrals for volume calculations
Solid conceptual understanding of calculus methods with clear justification for each mathematical choice
weaknesses: Minor algebraic transcription errors and occasional coefficient mismatches
Heavy reliance on graphing calculators/online tools without fully rigorous manual verification
No advanced extension into topics such as optimisation or formal error-propagation methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is fully commensurate with the course level and used comprehensively; understanding is thorough and mostly correct. Slight gaps in verification prevent the maximum, placing it in band 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally follow a logical sequence: data â interpolation â function â integral.  The reader can check how each volume is produced.  Occasional formatting glitches and overlong formulae hamper clarity, preventing a move to âexcellentâ, but the logical structure is still good, matching band 3.

Derivations and integrals show good conceptual understanding; calculated volumes are plausible and internal consistency is maintained.  Minor algebraic transcription errors and lack of rigorous checking of model fit introduce some doubt, so understanding is judged âgood and mostly correctâ rather than âthorough and completely correctâ, corresponding to band 4.

The student provides algebraic expressions, colour-coded graphs in Desmos, tabulated data points and a 3-D GeoGebra model â a good variety of representations that complement one another.  Quality is high but not polished to the point of âhighly effectiveâ because some tables are corrupted and a couple of figures lack scale labels.  Thus band 3 is most appropriate.

The exploration follows a clear linear narrative: background â modelling methodology â volume calculation â evaluation.  The reader can trace why each section of the bottle is isolated and how the five sub-functions combine into the final model, so logical development is evident.  Nevertheless, the report occasionally drifts into lengthy algebraic transcriptions and screenshots that interrupt the flow, and a few corrupted tables (e.g., Table 6) break coherence.  These issues prevent the work from reaching the âhighly coherent / excellentâ standard but still place it firmly in the âcoherent and logically developedâ band.

Creating separate polynomial fits, experimenting with additional points, and producing a 3-D model illustrate creativity and initiative beyond the minimum, but these steps follow known techniques and rely heavily on online calculators.  The strand therefore aligns with âgood level of creativity and initiativeâ (band 2).

There is thoughtful analysis of why a single global polynomial is unsuitable, discussion of errors introduced by perspective and missing grooves, and brief comparison with the stated 355 mL.  The analysis is competent but not âdeep and insightfulâ, so band 2 is justified.

The student evaluates the outcome by suggesting water-displacement for validation and acknowledges the inability to compute percentage error due to missing manufacturer data.  This constitutes a clear evaluation with implications but stops short of a comprehensive future plan, aligning with band 2.

The student reflects on measurement uncertainty, model assumptions (ignoring grooves) and the discrepancy between calculated and nominal volume, demonstrating meaningful reflection.  Reflection appears mainly in the final section rather than âthroughoutâ, so depth is adequate but not substantial, fitting band 2.

Variables, functions and integrals are written with correct symbols; domain restrictions are shown; Ï is factored out appropriately.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., cm vs m, copy-paste artefacts such as âÎâ) and one or two mis-typed coefficients reduce precision, so usage is âmostly consistentâ rather than flawless.

The narrative links personal nostalgia to the investigation and justifies methodological choices (piecewise vs single function).  This shows a clear personal approach, yet the exploration does not break new conceptual ground or introduce innovative data collection, so band 2 is appropriate.

Most content directly supports modelling the bottle or interpreting results, but there is noticeable repetition of the Lagrange formula and long algebraic expansions that do not assist understanding.  The student also includes screenshots of unsuccessful attempts that could have been summarised.  Consequently the work is generally relevant yet not fully concise, corresponding to band 3.

Polynomial interpolation, piecewise definition, definite integrals for volume and error considerations are all clearly relevant to the aim and employed effectively.  The mathematics is substantial but not explored with exhaustive rigour (e.g., no symbolic integration checks, heavy reliance on GDC), so ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ (band 4) best matches the evidence.

Choosing a familiar object, capturing a photograph, devising personal measurement techniques and deciding to use Lagrange rather than regression all demonstrate significant independent thinking, though the mathematical pathway remains standard for a modelling IA.  Evidence is stronger than âlimitedâ but not âoutstandingâ, placing it in band 2.

Headings, sub-headings, figures and tables create a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure, making it easy to locate information.  The five coloured subdivisions and sequential numbering of figures reinforce this structure.  However, several tables are mis-formatted and a few figures are referenced out of order, indicating that the organisation, while clear, is not exceptional.  Hence the strand fits the âwell organised with clear structureâ description.

The mathematics (degree-6 polynomials, numeric integration) is fully at HL level and appropriately selected for the context, satisfying âfully commensurate with course levelâ.  It is not extended to advanced topics such as spline fitting, optimisation or error propagation, hence band 4 rather than 5.

annotations.comment: Consistent figure numbering (e.g., Figure 5) across text and captions would improve organizational clarity.
Title clearly states the investigationâs focus and scope, establishing a strong professional presentation.
Personal narrative effectively links the studentâs childhood interest to the investigation, enhancing engagement.
Clear justification for using piecewise functions demonstrates solid understanding of interpolation behavior.
The decision to ignore grooves should be supported by a rough estimate of their volumetric impact to reinforce methodological assumptions.
Domain restrictions table is clear but its formatting and numbering should match the reportâs caption style for consistency.
Background description includes rich detail but could be more concise and directly connected to the modelling aim.
Figure 1 would benefit from axis labels and units to improve mathematical communication and clarity.
The coordinate table shows typographical artefacts (overlines, bars) that obscure numeric values. Reformat for clarity.
The Lagrange interpolation formula is accurately rendered in LaTeX, demonstrating effective use of mathematical notation.
Consider discussing the reliability and rounding error potential of the online interpolation calculator used.
Table 3 lacks clear headings and axis labels; add a title and labels for each column to improve presentation.
Check consistency: text lists y(1)=2.85 but equation uses 57/20; ensure values match exactly in description and calculation.
The detailed algebraic simplification is thorough but could be summarized to improve conciseness and reader focus.
The transition from initial to improved polynomial fit is well illustrated, demonstrating strong creative initiative.
Table 5âs header row is incomplete and misaligned; ensure full labels for X- and Y-coordinates to guide the reader.
Narrative explains the quality of fit succinctly, maintaining good logical structure and clarity.
Tables 6 and 7 are corrupted by OCR; manually re-enter these data tables to ensure accurate values are presented.
OCR errors have rendered the seven-point table nearly unreadable; re-enter and format manually for clarity.
When applying Lagrange with seven points, a concise algorithmic summary would reduce repetition and clarify methodology.
The expanded degree-6 polynomial coefficients are large fractions; include a note on verification to reinforce accuracy.
Final simplified form of fâ(x) is clearly presented, facilitating its subsequent use in volume calculations.
Quantify the maximum deviation of the initial cubic model from the bottle profile to justify adding additional data points.
Iterative refinement of fâ demonstrates strong independent thinking and initiative in tackling a complex section.
The theoretical basis for volume by revolution is well introduced, reinforcing the relevance of integral calculus.
Check unit consistency: uncertainty is stated as Â±0.05 m but volumes use cmÂ³; clarify units to avoid confusion.
Combining the volumes of shoulder and neck in a single calculation is efficient and maintains clarity.
The final piecewise function is clearly assembled, demonstrating excellent logical development and coherence.
Final numeric result for Vâbaseâ is clearly stated with units, demonstrating accurate use of mathematics.
Composite expressions for fâ and fâ are presented succinctly, aiding clear communication of multiple functions.
The 3D GeoGebra visualization enriches the exploration by offering a different representation of the bottleâs shape.
The evaluation proposes water displacement for validation, showing thoughtful analysis of outcomes and future work.
Evaluation section acknowledges methodological limitations and suggests concrete extensions, demonstrating meaningful reflection.
